title: Check Rack Tote Presence From RMS Map Color
summary: Use the RMS/container selection map view to determine whether a rack location currently has a tote or is empty by interpreting the rack color. The training source states that a dark blue or violet rack indicates a tote is present, while a light blue rack indicates the rack is empty.
when_to_use: Use this reference procedure when you need to determine tote presence at a rack location from the RMS/map container selection view, or when comparing displayed rack state to expected physical tote presence.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a corrective procedure for tote addition problems or other recovery actions; the source indicates those later steps are covered elsewhere.
Do not infer corrective actions from rack color alone.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Use the RMS/container selection map view to inspect rack locations and interpret rack color for tote presence state.

safety_notes: This runbook is limited to visual interpretation of rack state in the RMS/container selection view.
If displayed rack color does not match expected physical tote presence, stop using this reference as the sole basis for action and escalate or continue investigation.
access_or_tools_needed: Container selection view showing rack locations

access_required: Access to the RMS/container selection map view
system_preconditions: Rack locations must be visible in the RMS/map container selection view.

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing rack location and container lookup concepts, including rack color meaning in the map view.

steps.title: Open the RMS map and container selection view
steps.purpose: Access the map area where rack locations can be viewed and selected.
steps.instruction: Open the RMS map view and use the container selection area to view rack locations.
steps.expected_result: The rack locations are visible in the RMS/container selection view.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can see rack locations in the map and access the container selection area.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access the RMS/container selection view or rack locations are not visible.

actions.label: Open container selection map view
actions.exact_action: Click on the container selection and use it to view rack locations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen area showing rack locations and the container selection control.

stop_or_escalate_if: The RMS/container selection view cannot be accessed.
Rack locations are not visible in the map view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To look at more information about the rack, you have to click on the container selection and then physically click on the container itself.
Rack locations are shown in the RMS/map view. To inspect a rack, the operator uses container selection and clicks the rack/container location to view more information about that rack.

steps.title: Locate the rack position on the map
steps.purpose: Identify the specific rack location whose tote presence state needs to be checked.
steps.instruction: Locate the rack position you want to check on the map.
steps.expected_result: The target rack location is identified on the RMS map.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended rack location is clearly selected or visually identified in the map view.
steps.failure_condition: The target rack location cannot be found or distinguished on the map.

actions.label: Find target rack
actions.exact_action: Physically click on the container itself to inspect the rack location you want to check.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Rack locations in the map view that can be clicked or inspected.

stop_or_escalate_if: The intended rack location cannot be identified on the map.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To look at more information about the rack, you have to click on the container selection and then physically click on the container itself.
Rack locations are shown in the RMS/map view.

steps.title: Observe the rack color
steps.purpose: Read the visual state used by the system to indicate whether a tote is present.
steps.instruction: Observe the rack color and distinguish between the darker blue or violet tone and the lighter blue tone.
steps.expected_result: The rack color is identified as either dark blue/violet or light blue.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack color can be clearly distinguished as one of the two source-defined states.
steps.failure_condition: The rack color cannot be clearly distinguished or appears inconsistent with the expected visual states.

actions.label: Inspect rack color
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the rack has a dark blue or violet tone or a light blue tone.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Example of the two rack colors in the map view.

stop_or_escalate_if: The rack color cannot be reliably distinguished.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If a rack has this dark blue tone, it means it has a tote on it. If it has the light blue tone, it means it's empty.
Rack color in the RMS/map indicates tote presence. Dark blue or violet means a tote is on the rack. Light blue means the rack is empty.

steps.title: Interpret dark blue or violet as tote present
steps.purpose: Convert the observed dark blue/violet rack state into the correct tote presence meaning.
steps.instruction: Interpret a dark blue or violet rack as a rack with a tote present.
steps.expected_result: A dark blue or violet rack is understood to indicate tote presence.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack is dark blue or violet and is interpreted as occupied by a tote.
steps.failure_condition: A dark blue or violet rack is interpreted inconsistently or the displayed state appears to conflict with expected physical condition.

actions.label: Apply occupied-state meaning
actions.exact_action: If the rack has a dark blue tone or violet tone, treat it as a rack with a tote on it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Dark blue or violet rack example indicating tote present.

stop_or_escalate_if: The dark blue or violet rack state does not match expected physical tote presence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If a rack has this dark blue tone, it means it has a tote on it.
Dark blue or violet means a tote is on the rack.

steps.title: Interpret light blue as empty rack
steps.purpose: Convert the observed light blue rack state into the correct empty-rack meaning.
steps.instruction: Interpret a light blue rack as an empty rack with no tote present.
steps.expected_result: A light blue rack is understood to indicate no tote is present.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack is light blue and is interpreted as empty.
steps.failure_condition: A light blue rack is interpreted inconsistently or the displayed state appears to conflict with expected physical condition.

actions.label: Apply empty-state meaning
actions.exact_action: If the rack has the light blue tone, treat it as empty.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Light blue rack example indicating empty rack.

stop_or_escalate_if: The light blue rack state does not match expected physical tote presence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If it has the light blue tone, it means it's empty.
Light blue means the rack is empty.

steps.title: Compare displayed state to physical expectation if needed
steps.purpose: Identify mismatches between the RMS display and expected physical tote presence for further investigation.
steps.instruction: If needed, compare the displayed rack state to what is physically expected at that location, such as whether a tote should be present.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms whether the displayed rack state matches expected physical tote presence.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed rack color meaning matches what is physically expected at that location.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed rack color meaning does not match expected physical tote presence.

actions.label: Compare system state to expected physical state
actions.exact_action: Compare the displayed rack state to whether a tote is expected to be present at that location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Rack state display used to compare map indication against expected tote presence.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed rack color does not match the expected physical tote presence.
Further corrective action is needed, because this reference does not define recovery steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The instructor describes using these visual states to identify tote addition problems, missing totes, wrong totes at sorter destinations, and mismatches between physical labels and system state.

success_criteria: The user can determine from the map whether a rack location is shown as occupied by a tote or empty.
The user correctly interprets dark blue or violet as tote present.
The user correctly interprets light blue as empty.
healthy_conditions: Rack locations are visible in the RMS/container selection map view.
Dark blue or violet rack color is interpreted as tote present.
Light blue rack color is interpreted as empty.
Displayed rack state matches expected physical tote presence when compared.
failure_conditions: The RMS/container selection map view cannot be accessed.
The target rack location cannot be identified.
The rack color cannot be clearly distinguished.
Displayed rack color does not match expected physical tote presence.
A corrective or recovery action is needed beyond simple state interpretation.
escalation_guidance: Escalate or continue investigation if the displayed rack color does not match the expected physical tote presence.
Do not infer corrective actions from this runbook alone; the source says later steps for tote addition problems are covered elsewhere.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Container selection area, rack locations, and rack color examples used to determine tote presence.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If a rack has this dark blue tone, it means it has a tote on it. If it has the light blue tone, it means it's empty.
To look at more information about the rack, you have to click on the container selection and then physically click on the container itself.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for this procedure.
The source does not specify production stop requirements.
The source does not specify LOTO requirements.
The source does not define corrective actions when displayed rack state does not match physical expectation.
The packet includes additional related artifacts showing similar container selection content, but this candidate directly references only one artifact.
